proves: GPU-resident decode+prefill applies per-head QK-norm and (with untied output embeddings) is token+text identical to the pinned llama.cpp reference on the exact 3-prompt / 1,5,50-token support bar — the same bar the 8B row was promoted on.

support_bar_vs_llama_cpp.note: Resident 8B (CAMELID_METAL_RESIDENT_DECODE/PREFILL=1) chat generations vs the recorded llama.cpp reference_content_tokens from the merged qwen3-8b chatml-parity bundle (identical GGUF, sha256 408b9555...). 3 fixed ChatML thinking-disabled prompts at 1/5/50 tokens. Captured on mini2 (clean 16GB Mac mini M4).

rows.prompt: What is the capital of France?
What is the capital of France?
What is the capital of France?

large_context_engagement.note: A single user turn whose rendered ChatML prompt tokenizes to 7,982 tokens runs end-to-end on the resident path and produces coherent output. The resident decode trace confirms single-shot prefill: positions 0..7,980 filled in one pass, decode begins at pos 7,981 (no per-token seeding below the prompt length). A token-parity reference at 8B big-context is omitted because the f32 CPU reference prefill for an 8B model is ~8 min per 1k tokens (impractical on this hardware); the resident prefill *kernel* itself is proven bit-exact vs llama.cpp at 15,373 tokens on Qwen3-1.7B (sibling bundle), exercising the same code path, and the 8B-specific dims + untied embeddings are covered by the support-bar parity above.
